sglang.title: [AMD] Resolve the performance degression when launch server with "--enable-aiter-allreduce-fusion"
sglang.author: kkHuang-amd
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
…sed-rms kernel

Motivation

With --enable-aiter-allreduce-fusion, SGLang uses GroupCoordinator.fused_allreduce_rmsnorm, which may call custom_fused_ar_rms with a use_1stage_ar flag. For the default (non-deterministic) path, 1-stage is selected only when the payload is small (total_bytes <= 128 * 10

sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

The release-whl-kernel.yml workflow's build jobs were all skipped when triggered by a push to main (e.g. this run)
Root cause: each build job's if condition only checked github.event.inputs.target, which is null for push events — it's only populated for workflow_dispatch

Several ROCm/aiter post-processing paths were replacing existing weight objects with newly constructed ones after shuffle_weight(...). That drops custom attributes attached to the original parameters, such as weight_loader, and breaks RL workflows that call load_weights() again after mode

The test passed torch.bfloat16 (a torch.dtype object) as cache_dtype, but allocate_uniform_kv_caches expects a CacheDType string (e.g. "bfloat16")
This caused AttributeError: 'torch.dtype' object has no attribute 'start

The tools parameter was add

vllm.title: [Kernel] Mamba support different layout for Conv state
vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR proposes swapping the 2D layout of the Conv state from (state_len, dim) to (dim, state_len), similarly to the work done for NHD->HND layout for the same purposes of efficient indexing in PD.
This new layout leads to overall better performance (TTFT particularly) as well as enabling Heterogen

Phase 1 (of 2) for issue #35792.
Adds fused output quantization (FP8 static and NVFP4) for MLA attention, eliminating a separate quant kernel per layer by folding
quantization into the unified_mla_attention_with_output custom op.
Changes

mla_attention.py: Implement fused output quant in for

vllm.title: [Kernel] Fuse FP8 output quantization into merge_attn_states
vllm.author: carlyou
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Closes #33097

Add optional output_scale parameter to merge_attn_states (CUDA kernel, Triton kernel, Python bindings, dispatcher) for fused FP8 static per-tensor quantization
When output_scale is provided, the kernel quantizes merged attention output directly to FP8 during the final store, e

vllm.title: [Perf] Batch KV cache swap copies via cuMemcpyBatchAsync
vllm.author: Etelis
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Replace per-layer per-block swap_blocks calls in the KV cache offloading
handler with a single swap_blocks_batch call that submits all copies in
one driver invocation.
On CUDA 12.8+ this uses cuMemcpyBatchAsync; on older CUDA/ROCm it falls
back to a flat cudaMemcpyAsync loop with cudaMemcpyDefault.

This PR replaces hardcoded "cuda" device strings with dynamic platform checks across tests/v1 and tests/lora. By utilizing current_platform.device_type, we enable these test suites to be reused across different hardware accelerators (e.g., ROCm, Gaudi, XPU) without manual modification.

vllm.title: full cudagraph for flex-attn
vllm.author: shunting314
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Make vllm flex-attention backend support full cudagraph. Previously it only support piecewise cudagraphs.
There are mainly 2 reasons that full cudagraph does not work upfront for flex-attn

the warm-up run and the capture-run use different max-seq-len. That causes re-compilation during graph capture

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
build_connector_worker_meta() was not being called in the MRV2 code path.
PR #31964 added KVConnectorWorkerMetadata support, which covers MRV1 path in kv_connector_model_runner_mixin.py correctly, but the MRV2 path in gpu/kv_connector.py was missing this build_connector_worker_meta() call.
